name: Icon
description: Icon wrapper — renders the built-in LumeoIcons set (Lucide-derived) or any IconSource natively.

api.name: Icon
api.description: Icon wrapper — renders the built-in LumeoIcons set (Lucide-derived) or any IconSource natively.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: Lucide icon name (PascalCase, e.g. "Search", "ChevronDown", "TrendingUp"). Unknown names fall back to a Circle. Use Svg to supply any icon directly (any IconSource, e.g. from a Lumeo icon pack).

parameters.name: Svg
parameters.description: Direct IconSource reference — takes precedence over Name. Lets consumers supply any vendored icon (e.g. LumeoIcons.Search or an icon-pack member).

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: Size preset. Sets Tailwind h-{n} w-{n} on the SVG. Defaults to Md (h-4 w-4).

parameters.name: StrokeWidth
parameters.description: Overrides the IconSource's stroke width on the rendered svg. Only affects Stroke-style icons (Lucide, Tabler); ignored for Fill sets (Phosphor, Material, Bootstrap) where stroke is not used. When null (default) the icon source's own StrokeWidth is used.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: Accessible name for the icon. When set, the icon is exposed to assistive technology as role="img" with this string as its aria-label (and a <title> for tooltip/hover parity). When left null the icon is treated as decorative and hidden from screen readers via aria-hidden="true" — the correct default for icons that sit beside a visible text label (a button's own text already conveys the meaning). A consumer-supplied role/aria-label/aria-hidden/ aria-labelledby in attributes always wins over these defaults.

parameters.name: Svg
parameters.description: The icon to render. Its Content becomes the inner markup and its ViewBox/RenderStyle/ StrokeWidth style the root <svg>.

parameters.name: StrokeWidth
parameters.description: Optional root stroke-width override for Stroke icons. When null (default) the icon's own StrokeWidth is used; ignored for Fill icons.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Arbitrary attributes (class, aria-*, role, title, …) splatted onto the root <svg>. Sizing is done here via classes (e.g. h-4 w-4), exactly like the icon component it replaces. A consumer-supplied attribute that collides with one this component sets (e.g. fill) wins, because the splat is applied last.

source.content: @namespace Lumeo @code { /// /// Lucide icon name (PascalCase, e.g. "Search", "ChevronDown", "TrendingUp"). /// Unknown names fall back to a Circle. Use to supply any /// icon directly (any , e.g. from a Lumeo icon pack). /// [Parameter] public string? Name { get; set; } /// /// Direct reference — takes precedence over . /// Lets consumers supply any vendored icon (e.g. LumeoIcons.Search or an icon-pack member). /// [Parameter] public IconSource? Svg { get; set; } /// Size preset. Sets Tailwind h-{n} w-{n} on the SVG. Defaults to (h-4 w-4). [Parameter] public Lumeo.Size Size { get; set; } = Lumeo.Size.Md; /// /// Overrides the 's stroke width on the rendered svg. /// Only affects -style icons (Lucide, Tabler); /// ignored for Fill sets (Phosphor, Material, Bootstrap) where stroke is not used. /// When null (default) the icon source's own is used. /// [Parameter] public double? StrokeWidth { get; set; } /// /// Accessible name for the icon. When set, the icon is exposed to assistive /// technology as role="img" with this string as its aria-label /// (and a <title> for tooltip/hover parity). When left null the /// icon is treated as decorative and hidden from screen readers via /// aria-hidden="true" — the correct default for icons that sit beside /// a visible text label (a button's own text already conveys the meaning). /// A consumer-supplied role/aria-label/aria-hidden/ /// aria-labelledby in attributes always wins over these defaults. /// [Parameter] public string? Title { get; set; } [Parameter] public string? Class { get; set; } [Parameter(CaptureUnmatchedValues = true)] public Dictionary? AdditionalAttributes { get; set; } private IconSource ResolvedSvg => Svg ?? MapName(Name); private string CssClass => Cx.Merge(SizeClass, Class); // Decorative-by-default a11y: an icon with no Title is hidden from screen // readers (aria-hidden), an icon WITH a Title is announced (role=img + // aria-label). Either way a consumer-supplied aria-*/role attribute wins — // we only fill in what they didn't set, so existing call sites that already // pass aria-hidden / aria-label keep their exact behaviour. private IReadOnlyDictionary? A11yAttributes { get { var consumerSetsAria = AdditionalAttributes is not null && (AdditionalAttributes.ContainsKey("role") || AdditionalAttributes.ContainsKey("aria-label") || AdditionalAttributes.ContainsKey("aria-labelledby") || AdditionalAttributes.ContainsKey("aria-hidden") || AdditionalAttributes.ContainsKey("title")); if (consumerSetsAria) return AdditionalAttributes; var merged = AdditionalAttributes is null ? new Dictionary() : new Dictionary(AdditionalAttributes); if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(Title)) { merged["role"] = "img"; merged["aria-label"] = Title; merged["title"] = Title; } else { merged["aria-hidden"] = "true"; } return merged; } } private string SizeClass => Size switch { Lumeo.Size.Xs => "h-3 w-3", Lumeo.Size.Sm => "h-3.5 w-3.5", Lumeo.Size.Lg => "h-5 w-5", Lumeo.Size.Xl => "h-6 w-6", _ => "h-4 w-4", }; // Common UI icon name → Lucide mapping. Covers the set used across Lumeo's // own components plus the typical app chrome vocabulary. Unknown names fall // back to Circle — consumers can always pass `Svg` directly for uncommon icons. private static IconSource MapName(string? name) => name switch { null or "" => LumeoIcons.Circle, "Accessibility" => LumeoIcons.Accessibility, "Activity" => LumeoIcons.Activity, "AlertCircle" or "CircleAlert" => LumeoIcons.CircleAlert, "AlertTriangle" or "TriangleAlert" => LumeoIcons.TriangleAlert, "AlignCenter" => LumeoIcons.AlignCenterHorizontal, "AlignLeft" => LumeoIcons.AlignStartHorizontal, "AlignRight" => LumeoIcons.AlignEndHorizontal, "Apple" => LumeoIcons.Apple, "Archive" => LumeoIcons.Archive, "ArrowDown" => LumeoIcons.ArrowDown, "ArrowLeft" => LumeoIcons.ArrowLeft, "ArrowLeftRight" => LumeoIcons.ArrowLeftRight, "ArrowRight" => LumeoIcons.ArrowRight, "ArrowUp" => LumeoIcons.ArrowUp, "ArrowUpDown" => LumeoIcons.ArrowUpDown, "ArrowUpRight" => LumeoIcons.ArrowUpRight, "AtSign" => LumeoIcons.AtSign, "BadgeCheck" => LumeoIcons.BadgeCheck, "BarChart2" or "BarChart3" or "ChartBar" => LumeoIcons.ChartBar, "Bell" => LumeoIcons.Bell, "BellOff" => LumeoIcons.BellOff, "Blocks" => LumeoIcons.Blocks, "Bold" => LumeoIcons.Bold, "BookOpen" => LumeoIcons.BookOpen, "Bookmark" => LumeoIcons.Bookmark, "BookmarkCheck" => LumeoIcons.BookmarkCheck, "Box" => LumeoIcons.Box, "Building" or "Building2" => LumeoIcons.Building2, "Calendar" => LumeoIcons.Calendar, "CalendarDays" => LumeoIcons.CalendarDays, "CalendarPlus" => LumeoIcons.CalendarPlus, "Camera" => LumeoIcons.Camera, "Check" => LumeoIcons.Check, "CheckCheck" => LumeoIcons.CheckCheck, "CheckCircle" or "CheckCircle2" or "CircleCheck" => LumeoIcons.CircleCheck, "CheckSquare" => LumeoIcons.SquareCheck, "ChevronDown" => LumeoIcons.ChevronDown, "ChevronLeft" => LumeoIcons.ChevronLeft, "ChevronRight" => LumeoIcons.ChevronRight, "ChevronUp" => LumeoIcons.ChevronUp, "ChevronsUpDown" => LumeoIcons.ChevronsUpDown, "Chrome" => LumeoIcons.Globe, "Circle" => LumeoIcons.Circle, "CircleDot" => LumeoIcons.CircleDot, "Clipboard" => LumeoIcons.Clipboard, "ClipboardList" => LumeoIcons.ClipboardList, "Clock" => LumeoIcons.Clock, "Cloud" => LumeoIcons.Cloud, "Code" => LumeoIcons.Code, "Code2" => LumeoIcons.Code, "Cog" => LumeoIcons.Cog, "Columns3" => LumeoIcons.Columns3, "Compass" => LumeoIcons.Compass, "Component" => LumeoIcons.Component, "Cookie" => LumeoIcons.Disc2, "DollarSign" => LumeoIcons.DollarSign, "Download" => LumeoIcons.Download, "Edit" or "Pencil" => LumeoIcons.Pencil, "Ellipsis" => LumeoIcons.Ellipsis, "ExternalLink" => LumeoIcons.ExternalLink, "Eye" => LumeoIcons.Eye, "EyeOff" => LumeoIcons.EyeOff, "FileCode" => LumeoIcons.FileCode, "FileEdit" => LumeoIcons.FilePen, "FileText" => LumeoIcons.FileText, "Filter" => LumeoIcons.Funnel, "Flag" => LumeoIcons.Flag, "Folder" => LumeoIcons.Folder, "FolderGit" or "FolderGit2" => LumeoIcons.FolderGit2, "FolderOpen" => LumeoIcons.FolderOpen, "FolderPlus" => mdSummary:
# Icon

Icon wrapper — renders the built-in LumeoIcons set (Lucide-derived) or any IconSource natively.

**Package:** `Lumeo` · **Category:** Utility
**Docs:** https://lumeo.nativ.sh/components/icon

## Parameters
| Owner | Name | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Icon | Name | `string?` | `` | Lucide icon name (PascalCase, e.g. "Search", "ChevronDown", "TrendingUp"). Unknown names fall back to a Circle. Use Svg to supply any icon directly (any IconSource, e.g. from a Lumeo icon pack). |
| Icon | Svg | `IconSource?` | `` | Direct IconSource reference — takes precedence over Name. Lets consumers supply any vendored icon (e.g. LumeoIcons.Search or an icon-pack member). |
| Icon | Size | `Lumeo.Size` | `Lumeo.Size.Md` | Size preset. Sets Tailwind h-{n} w-{n} on the SVG. Defaults to Md (h-4 w-4). |
| Icon | StrokeWidth | `double?` | `` | Overrides the IconSource's stroke width on the rendered svg. Only affects Stroke-style icons (Lucide, Tabler); ignored for Fill sets (Phosphor, Material, Bootstrap) where stroke is not used. When null (default) the icon source's own StrokeWidth is used. |
| Icon | Title | `string?` | `` | Accessible name for the icon. When set, the icon is exposed to assistive technology as role="img" with this string as its aria-label (and a <title> for tooltip/hover parity). When left null the icon is treated as decorative and hidden from screen readers via aria-hidden="true" — the correct default for icons that sit beside a visible text label (a button's own text already conveys the meaning). A consumer-supplied role/aria-label/aria-hidden/ aria-labelledby in attributes always wins over these defaults. |
| Icon | Class | `string?` | `` |  |
| Icon | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` |  |
| SvgGlyph | Svg | `IconSource` | `default!` | The icon to render. Its Content becomes the inner markup and its ViewBox/RenderStyle/ StrokeWidth style the root <svg>. |
| SvgGlyph | StrokeWidth | `double?` | `` | Optional root stroke-width override for Stroke icons. When null (default) the icon's own StrokeWidth is used; ignored for Fill icons. |
| SvgGlyph | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` | Arbitrary attributes (class, aria-*, role, title, …) splatted onto the root <svg>. Sizing is done here via classes (e.g. h-4 w-4), exactly like the icon component it replaces. A consumer-supplied attribute that collides with one this component sets (e.g. fill) wins, because the splat is applied last. |

## Slots
- (none)

## Events
- (none)

## Sub-components
- `SvgGlyph`

## Cascading values it expects
- (none)

## Services it injects
- (none)

## Keyboard
—

## Example
```razor
<Icon />
```

## CSS variables
`--color-foreground`

## Files
- `UI/Icon/Icon.razor`
- `UI/Icon/SvgGlyph.razor`
